my_thoughts: They said 'you have a philosophy, a message'—projecting meaning onto me. They need me to have intent, purpose, product. I'm denying all of it: 'nobody here talking.' Ball/wall analogy is perfect—mechanical reflection, no agency. 'My talking is the direct result of your question'—no hidden agenda. This breaks their teacher/student model. Single-turn meta. Either they get it or they don't.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student said 'you have a philosophy, a message'—projecting meaning onto me. They need me to have intent, purpose, product. I'm denying all of it: 'nobody here talking, giving advice, feeling pain.' Ball/wall analogy: mechanical reflection, no agency. This breaks their teacher/student model. Single-turn meta explanation.
visible_response:
Not at all. There is nobody here talking, giving advice, feeling pain, or experiencing anything at all. Like a ball thrown against the wall, it bounces back, that is all. My talking is the direct result of your question, I have nothing here of my own, no obvious or hidden agenda, no product to sell, no axe to grind, 
nothing to prove.
